ucs.title: [LLM] ZIRCONIUM/APT31 Dropbox API C2 & exfil from non-browser LOLBin (DropboxAES / RPipeCommander)
ucs.description: Hunts the signature ZIRCONIUM (Microsoft: Violet Typhoon) C2 and exfiltration channel where their custom backdoors (DropboxAES, jian/RPipeCommander) abuse the legitimate Dropbox API as a bidirectional command channel. Detection fires when a Dropbox API endpoint is contacted by an interpreter or LOLBin rather than the genuine Dropbox client or a user browser — a chain APT31 has used in operations against US election staff, EU/Italian/Finnish government targets, and in the Check Point 'Jian' (EpMe/EquationGroup-derived) tooling deployments.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_host) as dest_host values(All_Traffic.user) as user values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_host="*.dropboxapi.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*content.dropboxapi.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="api-content.dropbox.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="notify.dropboxapi.com") AND All_Traffic.app IN ("msiexec.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","python.exe","pythonw.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port 
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` 
| where bytes_out > 1024 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` 
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` 
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// ZIRCONIUM / APT31 Dropbox API C2 + exfil from LOLBin / interpreter
// T1102.002 + T1567.002 + T1573.001 — DropboxAES / RPipeCommander tradecraft
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _dropbox_api_hosts = dynamic([
    "api.dropboxapi.com", "content.dropboxapi.com",
    "api-content.dropbox.com", "notify.dropboxapi.com"
]);
let _legit_dropbox_clients = dynamic([
    "dropbox.exe", "dropboxupdate.exe",
    "msedge.exe", "chrome.exe", "firefox.exe", "brave.exe",
    "outlook.exe", "onedrive.exe", "msteams.exe", "explorer.exe",
    "slack.exe", "zoom.exe"
]);
let _suspect_initiators = dynamic([
    "msiexec.exe", "rundll32.exe", "regsvr32.exe",
    "powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe", "cmd.exe",
    "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe", "mshta.exe",
    "python.exe", "pythonw.exe", "curl.exe", "certutil.exe"
]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (_dropbox_api_hosts)
   or RemoteUrl endswith "dropboxapi.com"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_legit_dropbox_clients)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_suspect_initiators)
      or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\")
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// ZIRCONIUM / APT31 Dropbox API C2 from LOLBin via ASIM (cross-vendor)
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _legit_dropbox_clients = dynamic([
    "dropbox.exe", "dropboxupdate.exe",
    "msedge.exe", "chrome.exe", "firefox.exe", "brave.exe",
    "outlook.exe", "onedrive.exe", "msteams.exe", "explorer.exe",
    "slack.exe", "zoom.exe"
]);
let _suspect_initiators = dynamic([
    "msiexec.exe", "rundll32.exe", "regsvr32.exe",
    "powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe", "cmd.exe",
    "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe", "mshta.exe",
    "python.exe", "pythonw.exe", "curl.exe", "certutil.exe"
]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where (DstHostname endswith "dropboxapi.com")
     or (Url has "dropboxapi.com")
| extend ProcImg = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ProcImg !in~ (_legit_dropbox_clients)
| where ProcImg in~ (_suspect_initiators)
| where SrcUsername !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, SrcIpAddr, DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber,
          DstHostname, Url, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, SrcUsername
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: ZIRCONIUM/APT31 Dropbox API C2 from Non-Browser LOLBin
id: 9c2b5e1a-7f4d-4b6c-8a9c-3a1f8d5e0a01
status: experimental
description: Detects ZIRCONIUM (APT31 / Violet Typhoon) DropboxAES / RPipeCommander backdoor C2 and exfiltration via the Dropbox API where the connecting process is a Windows LOLBin or interpreter rather than the genuine Dropbox client or a user browser.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0128/
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2021/jian-the-chinese-double-edged-cyber-sword/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.exfiltration
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.g0128
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_dest:
    DestinationHostname|endswith:
      - '.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'api-content.dropbox.com'
      - 'notify.dropboxapi.com'
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\python.exe'
      - '\pythonw.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  condition: selection_dest and selection_proc
falsepositives:
  - Internal automation that intentionally pushes to Dropbox via PowerShell/python (allowlist by host + script hash)
level: high
ucs.rationale: ZIRCONIUM/APT31 is one of the few state actors whose tooling (DropboxAES, jian/RPipeCommander, the backdoor seen in the Check Point Jian and Microsoft 2021 election-targeting reports) consistently abuses the Dropbox API for both bidirectional C2 (T1102.002) and exfiltration (T1567.002) — a way to hide infrastructure (T1665) behind a legitimate cloud service. Filtering on Dropbox-API hostnames PLUS

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActorUsername` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImAuthentication, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

ucs.title: [LLM] ZIRCONIUM/APT31 spearphishing-link → msiexec /i HTTP install chain
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical APT31 delivery sequence: a spearphishing email with a link (T1566.002), the recipient clicking it (T1204.001), and within minutes msiexec.exe being invoked with a remote HTTP(S) MSI URL (T1218.007) — the exact pattern Microsoft documented for ZIRCONIUM operations against the 2020 US election campaign staff and that Check Point observed delivering APT31 implants. The HTTP-MSI invocation by msiexec is itself rare; pairing it with a preceding Safe Links click on the same recipient is high-fidelity.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_id) as process_id values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_process_name values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=msiexec.exe (Processes.process="*/i http*" OR Processes.process="*/i https*" OR Processes.process="*/package http*") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| where match(parent_process_name, "(?i)(chrome|msedge|firefox|brave|outlook|explorer|iexplore)\.exe$") 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` 
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` 
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// ZIRCONIUM / APT31 phishing-link -> msiexec /i HTTP(S) install
// T1566.002 -> T1204.001 -> T1218.007 (canonical 2020 election-targeting chain)
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 600;     // 10-minute click-to-execution window
// 1. Inbound delivered email + click on its URL
let SuspectClicks =
    EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound" and DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
    | join kind=inner (
        EmailUrlInfo | project NetworkMessageId, MailUrl = Url, UrlDomain
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | join kind=inner (
        UrlClickEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
        | where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed", "ClickedThrough")
        | project NetworkMessageId, ClickTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn,
                  IsClickedThrough, ClickIp = IPAddress
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | project NetworkMessageId, ClickTime, AccountUpn, MailUrl, UrlDomain,
              Subject, SenderFromAddress, IsClickedThrough;
// 2. msiexec installing from a remote HTTP(S) URL within the window
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s/(i|package)\s+https?://"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner SuspectClicks on $left.AccountUpn == $right.AccountUpn
| where Timestamp between (ClickTime .. ClickTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, ClickTime),
         MsiUrl   = extract(@"(?i)https?://[^\s\"']+", 0, ProcessCommandLine)
| project ClickTime, ProcessTime = Timestamp, DelaySec,
          DeviceName, AccountUpn,
          SenderFromAddress, Subject, MailUrl, UrlDomain, IsClickedThrough,
          MsiUrl, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by ClickTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// ZIRCONIUM / APT31 msiexec-from-URL (Sentinel: process-only; Safe Links
// click data has no native Sentinel equivalent, so we anchor on the
// msiexec /i HTTP(S) command line + browser/Outlook/Explorer parent.)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith @"\msiexec.exe"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s/(i|package)\s+https?://"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentImg = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ParentImg in~ (
    "explorer.exe", "chrome.exe", "msedge.exe", "firefox.exe",
    "brave.exe", "outlook.exe", "iexplore.exe"
  )
| extend MsiUrl = extract(@"(?i)https?://[^\s\"']+", 0, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, MsiUrl, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Microsoft Threat Intelligence (then MSTIC, now MSTIC under the Violet Typhoon name) and Check Point both publicly attributed the spearphishing-link → remote-MSI install pattern to ZIRCONIUM/APT31 — most prominently against US 2020 election personnel and against international-affairs targets. msiexec.exe being launched with /i and a literal HTTP(S) URL is rare in benign environments (legitimate ins

_field_issues.message: `DelaySec` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents, EmailEvents, EmailUrlInfo, UrlClickEvents.
`MsiUrl` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents, EmailEvents, EmailUrlInfo, UrlClickEvents. Did you mean `Url`?

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `MsiUrl` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
